ucs.title: [LLM] RTM banking trojan deploying LiteManager/RMS (Remote Manipulator System) as covert RAT
ucs.description: Hunts for execution of LiteManager / Remote Utilities (rutserv.exe, rfusclient.exe) outside the vendor install path. RTM (G0048) characteristically drops a hidden, repackaged build of Russian-made RMS/LiteManager as its hands-on-keyboard RAT against Russian/CIS accounting and remote-banking (1C, RBS) operators rather than commodity Cobalt Strike, making this binary combination a near-signature artefact of the crew.
ucs.splunk: | tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where (Processes.process_name IN ("rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe","rmsservice.exe") OR Processes.original_file_name IN ("rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe") OR Processes.process IN ("*LiteManager*","*Remote Manipulator*","*Remote Utilities*","*TektonIT*")) by Processes.dest Processes.user Processes.process_name Processes.process_path Processes.parent_process_name Processes.parent_process_path Processes.process Processes.process_hash | `drop_dm_object_name(Processes)` | where NOT (process_path IN ("C:\\Program Files\\LiteManager*","C:\\Program Files (x86)\\LiteManager*","C:\\Program Files\\Remote Utilities*","C:\\Program Files (x86)\\Remote Utilities*")) | join type=outer dest [| tstats summariesonly=true count from datamodel=Endpoint.Registry where Registry.registry_path="*\\Microsoft\\Windows\\CurrentVersion\\Run*" Registry.registry_value_data IN ("*rutserv*","*rfusclient*","*rmsservice*") by Registry.dest Registry.registry_path Registry.registry_value_data | `drop_dm_object_name(Registry)`] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)`
ucs.kql:
let suspectRMS = dynamic(["rutserv.exe","rfusclient.exe","rmsservice.exe"]);
let rmsCompanies = dynamic(["TektonIT","LiteManager","Remote Utilities"]);
DeviceProcessEvents
| where Timestamp > ago(30d)
| where FileName in~ (suspectRMS)
   or ProcessVersionInfoOriginalFileName in~ (suspectRMS)
   or ProcessVersionInfoCompanyName has_any (rmsCompanies)
   or ProcessVersionInfoProductName has_any (rmsCompanies)
| where not(FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files\LiteManager"
          or FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\LiteManager"
          or FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files\Remote Utilities"
          or FolderPath startswith @"C:\Program Files (x86)\Remote Utilities")
| extend SuspiciousPath = (FolderPath startswith @"C:\Users\" or FolderPath startswith @"C:\ProgramData\" or FolderPath startswith @"C:\Windows\Temp\" or FolderPath startswith @"C:\Intel\" or FolderPath startswith @"C:\PerfLogs\")
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceRegistryEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where RegistryKey has @"\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Run"
    | where RegistryValueData has_any ("rutserv","rfusclient","rmsservice")
    | project DeviceId, RegPersistKey=RegistryKey, RegPersistValue=RegistryValueData
) on DeviceId
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, AccountName, FileName, FolderPath, SHA256, ProcessCommandLine, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, SuspiciousPath, RegPersistKey, RegPersistValue
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: LiteManager/RMS is a legitimate Russian remote-admin product but is rarely deployed by Western enterprises and almost never from %APPDATA%, %ProgramData% or %TEMP%. RTM specifically renames/repacks rutserv.exe and persists it via Run keys; pairing the binary fingerprint (TektonIT signer / original filename) with non-vendor install path filters out IT-admin usage and isolates the RTM tradecraft cha

ucs.title: [LLM] RTM C2 dead-drop resolution via Namecoin .bit TLD and Pastebin/Livejournal hardcoded profiles
ucs.description: Hunts for the RTM Trojan's distinctive dead-drop resolver chain: DNS lookups for *.bit (Namecoin blockchain) domains and/or HTTP fetches to hardcoded Livejournal/Pastebin author pages used to retrieve the live C2 IP. Almost no other tracked actor combines .bit-TLD resolution with Livejournal as a fallback resolver.
ucs.splunk: (| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Network_Resolution.DNS where (DNS.query="*.bit" OR DNS.query="*.bazar") by DNS.src DNS.query DNS.answer DNS.dest | `drop_dm_object_name(DNS)` | eval source_type="namecoin_bit") | append [| tstats summariesonly=true count min(_time) as firstTime max(_time) as lastTime from datamodel=Web.Web where (Web.url="*livejournal.com/users/*" OR Web.url="*pastebin.com/raw/*" OR Web.url="*livejournal.com/data/*") AND Web.user_agent!="*Mozilla*" by Web.src Web.dest Web.url Web.user_agent Web.app | `drop_dm_object_name(Web)` | eval source_type="deaddrop_paste"] | join type=outer src [| tstats summariesonly=true values(Processes.process_name) as proc values(Processes.process_path) as proc_path from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes where Processes.process_name IN ("*.exe","rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") by Processes.dest | rename Processes.dest as src | fields src proc proc_path] | `security_content_ctime(firstTime)` | `security_content_ctime(lastTime)` | table firstTime lastTime src dest source_type query url user_agent answer proc proc_path count
ucs.kql:
let bitLookups = DeviceEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where ActionType == "DnsQueryResponse" or ActionType == "InboundConnectionAccepted" or ActionType == "ConnectionSuccess"
    | extend QueryName = tostring(parse_json(AdditionalFields).QueryName)
    | where QueryName endswith ".bit" or QueryName endswith ".bazar"
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, QueryName, Source="namecoin_bit";
let pasteResolvers = DeviceNetworkEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where (RemoteUrl has "livejournal.com/users/" or RemoteUrl has "pastebin.com/raw/" or RemoteUrl has "livejournal.com/data/")
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName !in~ ("chrome.exe","msedge.exe","firefox.exe","iexplore.exe","opera.exe","brave.exe")
    | project Timestamp, DeviceName, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, Source="deaddrop_paste";
union bitLookups, pasteResolvers
| join kind=leftouter (
    DeviceProcessEvents
    | where Timestamp > ago(30d)
    | where InitiatingProcessFileName in~ ("rundll32.exe","regsvr32.exe") or ProcessCommandLine has_any ("DllRegisterServer","core.dll","client.dll")
    | project DeviceName, RTM_LoaderProc=FileName, RTM_LoaderCmd=ProcessCommandLine, ProcTime=Timestamp
) on DeviceName
| where isnull(ProcTime) or abs(datetime_diff('minute', Timestamp, ProcTime)) <= 60
| project Timestamp, DeviceName, Source, QueryName, RemoteUrl, RemoteIP, InitiatingProcessFileName, InitiatingProcessFolderPath, InitiatingProcessCommandLine, RTM_LoaderProc, RTM_LoaderCmd
| order by Timestamp desc
ucs.rationale: Namecoin .bit resolution is virtually unused in legitimate enterprise traffic and is a documented RTM-specific signal (ESET 'Read The Manual' 2017/2019 reports). Pairing .bit lookups OR non-browser HTTP to known Livejournal/Pastebin RTM resolver pages, joined to non-browser initiating processes (rundll32/regsvr32 loading core.dll — the RTM main module), tightly fingerprints the RTM Trojan's resolv
